sprint: Lumo Voice Batch: Sky-Recognition Challenge (4 lines) + Gate Flying/Landing (2 lines)
task: Wires 6 real recorded Lumo voice clips into js/lumoVoice.js and their call sites, uploaded directly to GitHub (assets/lumo/voice/lumo-18 through lumo-23.mp3) after a Bash file-copy permission rejection blocked the original plan -- this session's own working direct-upload pattern. 4 lines cover the Creator Signature sky-recognition challenge in js/magicCardUI.js: the opening prompt ('One of these skies is yours...'), a wrong tap ('Not quite...'), the fresh-decoys reshuffle ('Here's a new look...'), and success ('There it is! Welcome back.') -- each wired at its existing setMood() call site, verified via Playwright to fire the correct id at the correct moment with real, ffprobe-measured durations matching the module's own duration table. 2 more cover the Traveller Gateway's single Gate video (js/gatewaySequence.js): 'lumoFlying' fires as Segment 1 starts (the footage's own first ~2s is doors alone, then Lumo becomes visible flying in), 'lumoLanding' fires the instant Segment 1 pauses (Lumo has landed with the Story Egg between its feet) -- followed by a new LUMO_LANDING_HOLD_MS (2900ms) settle before the in-person greeting/recognition lines begin, so the two don't talk over each other. Verified via Playwright: both new Gateway beats fire in the correct order with correct durations on a fresh Traveller boot; full regression across the existing sky-challenge suites (single/multi-card flows, wrong-tap/reshuffle/success, the choice screen, the untouched cross-device Recall path) passes unchanged; both goldenBuild.js suites (World Builder v1 and v2) pass unchanged.
